title: 6 Redeeming Coupon, Fully Registered, and Special Fully Registered Canada Savings Bonds (Series 1â31)
body_text:
6	Redeeming Coupon, Fully Registered, and Special Fully Registered Canada Savings Bonds (Series 1â31)
6.1	Determining the Redemption Rate
All old-style Canada Savings Bonds (i.e., Series 1â31) have matured. When redeeming bonds, do not detach Coupons, compound interest certificates, or interim bonus corners.
Since old-style Canada Savings Bonds do not have a MICR, they cannot be processed on the Branch Capture application and must be manually processed by Central 1. Credit unions can send their old-style Canada Savings Bonds to Central 1 by courier or armoured car as per the credit unionâs internal policy for transporting negotiable items.
Coupon Bonds
The redemption value of a coupon-style Canada Savings Bond is determined by adding the face value to the Coupon value, maturity bonus, compound interest certificates, and interim cash bonuses as detailed in the Bank of Canada Unclaimed Properties Officeâs Online Tool or the Redemption Value Tables (S40).
Ensure all remaining Coupons, compound interest certificates, and interim bonus corners remain attached to the bond.
Note:	Compound interest certificates payable on some series are only applicable if the appropriate block of Coupons is attached.
Redeem any loose Coupons. See Chapter 8, Redeeming Government of Canada and Canada Savings Bonds Coupons, for more information.
The following image shows an example of a coupon-style Canada Savings Bond.
Fully Registered Bonds
Fully Registered Bonds are redeemed at the value indicated in the Bank of Canada Unclaimed Properties Officeâs Online Tool or the Redemption Value Tables (S40). The Bank of Canada pays the interest on Fully Registered Bonds to the Registered Owner by cheque or direct deposit.
The following image shows an example of a Fully Registered Bond.
Special Fully Registered Bonds
Interest on Special Fully Registered Bonds was compounded to maturity. Determine the redemption value from the Bank of Canada Unclaimed Properties Officeâs Online Tool or the Redemption Value Tables (S40).
Note:	Canada Savings Bonds series 22, 23, 25, 26, 27, and 28 offered interim cash bonus payments. If the upper left-hand corner of the bond has not been removed, the interim bonus has not been paid and must be included in the redemption value of the bond.
6.2	Processing Redemptions
To redeem old-style (i.e., Series 1â31) Canada Savings Bonds proceed as follows.
1.
Determine the redemption value of the bond.
2.
Have the bondholder sign the face of the bond. If the bondholder is not a member, obtain and record identification.
3.
Stamp the face of the bond with the credit unionâs Endorsement Stamp.
4.
Prepare tax statement(s) (i.e., T600) as stipulated in the Redemption Value Tables (S40). See Section 4.8, Preparing Tax Statements.
See Section 9.2, Preparing the Deposit for Central 1, for further information.
END
